act_name_th: ประมวลกฎหมายที่ดิน
amendments: As amended by Land Code Amendment Act (No. 8) B.E. 2542 (1999)

86.text_en:
Section 86. Aliens may acquire land by virtue of the provisions of a treaty giving them the right to own immovable properties, and subject to the provisions of this Code.
The extent of land to be acquired shall be within the limit prescribed by the Ministerial Regulation, but the area of land to be used for residential purposes shall not exceed one rai.

86/1.amendment_notes: Added by Land Code Amendment Act (No. 8) B.E. 2542 (1999). The 40 million Baht investment threshold has been discussed for potential reduction but remains unchanged as of 2025.

93.text_en: Section 93. The Minister shall permit the inheritance of land by an alien who is the lawful heir, but such acquisition, when added to land that is already held, shall not exceed the amount which may be held under Section 87.

94.text_en: Section 94. All land which an alien has acquired unlawfully or without permission shall be disposed of by such alien within the time limit prescribed by the Director-General, which shall not be less than one hundred and eighty days nor more than one year. If the land is not disposed of within the time prescribed, the Director-General shall have the power to dispose of it.

96.text_en: Section 96. When it appears that any person, including a juristic person, has acquired land as the owner in place of an alien or a juristic person under the provisions of Section 97 and Section 98, the Director-General shall have the authority to dispose of such land, and the provisions of Section 94 shall apply mutatis mutandis.

96 bis.text_en:
Section 96 bis. The provisions prescribing the acquisition of land by aliens by virtue of the provisions of a treaty under the first paragraph of Section 86 shall not apply to aliens who bring in capital for investment of not less than forty million Baht as prescribed in the Ministerial Regulation, provided that the acquisition of land for the purpose of residence shall not exceed one rai and shall be approved by the Minister.
If the alien fails to comply with the conditions prescribed in the Ministerial Regulation, the provisions of Section 94 shall apply mutatis mutandis.
96 bis.amendment_notes: Added by Land Code Amendment Act (No. 8) B.E. 2542 (1999). Note: Section 96 bis and Section 86/1 address overlapping subject matter; 86/1 provides the more detailed conditions.

98.text_en: Section 98. Juristic persons formed under foreign law shall be deemed to be aliens for the purpose of this Code.
